description:
Goal: move the shared native pipeline onto Compiler2-owned backend artifacts. Signal: JIT and AOT consume Compiler2 backend IR plus settled emission facts directly and never rebuild PlannedProgram or AbiFacts on the side.

Change:
- Teach the shared native codegen path to consume the backend IR and emission facts produced by Compiler2.
- Remove Compiler2 dependence on prepare_preplanned_native, materialize_program, AbiFacts::derive, and old planner callable-entry/native/continuation inventories.
- Feed callable entries, return ABI, param/value reprs, extern marshal classes, and effects from Compiler2 artifacts into the shared JIT/AOT driver.
- Add JIT/AOT contract tests for quicksort, Enum.reduce, and variadic extern through the Compiler2 path.

Why:
- Native codegen is the heaviest backend consumer and the main place old planner/codegen fact synthesis still hides.

Checks:
- Compiler2 JIT/AOT paths do not call prepare_preplanned_native, materialize_program, or AbiFacts::derive.
- Native runs preserve the settled callable-entry, marshal, return-lane, and effect contracts from EmissionReadyProgram.
title: Compiler2 backends: run JIT and AOT from Compiler2 artifacts
